description: Corrected external replication using cross-DATASET at BEST LAYER design (matches LODO protocol). Original cross-layer design was invalid for non-fine-tuned LMs.

design_rationale.problem_with_cross_layer: Different layers in an autoregressive LM serve different functional roles (next-token prediction vs semantic representation). kappa-logit relationship across layers is non-monotone. This does NOT match the LOAO design.
design_rationale.correct_design: For each dataset, find the best single layer (max 1-NN accuracy). Use that best-layer (kappa, logit_q) point. Fit alpha across datasets. This matches the LODO (leave-one-dataset-out) cross-dataset slope measurement.
design_rationale.connection_to_paper: LODO alpha=3.598 in comprehensive universality (19 archs x 10 datasets). Pre-registered LOAO interval [2.43, 3.29] also applicable since both measure the same law constant.

frozen_constants.source: 12-architecture LOAO BEFORE seeing SmolLM2 results. No post-hoc refit.

layer_selection: Best layer = layer with maximum 1-NN q_raw accuracy, scanning all 24 layers

PR3.label: clinc_oos validity
PR3.criterion: clinc_oos (K=151, new domain) has kappa > 0 and q_norm > 0.10

overall_pass_criterion: PR1 AND PR2 (both must pass for overall PASS)
